subject: Geography
title: Treasure Maps and Phantom Islands
description: A fast breakdown of Treasure Maps and Phantom Islands for builders.

hook.text: For centuries, maps showed islands that didn't exist—phantom islands born from fog and rumor.

buildup.text: The island of Hy-Brasil appeared on maps for 500 years before anyone confirmed it wasn't real.

discovery.text: Sandy Island appeared on maps and even Google Earth until 2012, when a ship sailed right through it.

twist.text: Some mapmakers placed fake features on purpose to catch plagiarists who copied their work.

climax.text: These 'trap streets' and fake towns still appear in modern maps and databases.

punchline.text: Maps contain lies on purpose—and nobody notices.

quiz.question: Why do some mapmakers include fake features?
options: To catch people who copy their maps
